key_insight: SGD can self-stabilize near the edge of stability, alternating between oscillatory and stable phases while still converging with large learning rates.
engineering_relevance: This suggests practitioners can use larger stepsizes in deep learning without immediate divergence, potentially improving speed and robustness when tuned carefully.
method: Analyze SGD on multiclass cross-entropy for linear classifiers and two-layer neural networks.
Prove fixed-time return to stability after edge-of-stability excursions.
Establish best-iterate convergence guarantees under large learning rates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments that validate the theory and show benefits of SGD in the large-stepsize regime.
watch_item: The guarantees are problem-specific and rely on cross-entropy models, so direct transfer to other losses or architectures may require new analysis.
paper_title: SGD at the Edge of Stability: Stochastic Stabilization with Large Learning Rates
